why_it_may_help: Genre/category may strongly affect bestseller shelf life and target audience.
Different NYT lists behave differently; children's books, fiction, and nonfiction may have different staying power.
Length may separate children's books, novels, nonfiction, and reference-style books.
Publication timing may matter because some genres are seasonal.
Richer metadata could correlate with better match quality or more commercially supported releases.
Reader engagement could help, but missingness is very high so these should be used carefully.
Series books may have built-in audiences and repeat demand.
Author productivity/popularity proxy; prolific authors may have more existing audience awareness.
More editions can proxy for popularity, longevity, translations, or reprints.
Availability/access fields may signal older/public/archived books versus newer commercial releases.
